steps.title: Understand the opening modifier
steps.content:
The sentence opens with "A government body officially known as the Althing," — an appositive phrase that renames a noun. The subject immediately following the comma must name the same entity as that phrase (Iceland's parliament = the Althing), or the modifier dangles and misidentifies what the Althing is.
  • Choice A — 'the world's oldest parliaments include one which first met in 930 CE, Iceland's.': Places "the world's oldest parliaments" as the subject, suggesting the Althing is the world's oldest parliaments (plural). Dangling modifier. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Iceland's parliament is one of the oldest in the world, first meeting in 930 CE.': Places "Iceland's parliament" directly after the comma. This correctly identifies Iceland's parliament as the government body known as the Althing, and the participial phrase "first meeting in 930 CE" adds detail without confusion. ✓
  • Choice C — 'the first meeting of one of the oldest parliaments in the world, Iceland's, was in 930 CE.': Places "the first meeting" as the subject, implying the Althing is a meeting rather than a parliament. Dangling modifier. ✗
  • Choice D — 'CE was the year when Iceland's parliament, one of the oldest parliaments in the world, first met.': Places "CE" as the subject, implying the Althing is a year. Dangling modifier. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Only choice B places the correct noun — Iceland's parliament — immediately after the opening appositive, avoiding a dangling modifier. That matches choice B — B is correct.
